sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen are struggling, having lost three of their last five matches, including a 4-1 defeat to VfB Stuttgart. SV Werder Bremen are also in poor form, winless in their last eight games, though they managed a 3-3 draw against Eintracht Frankfurt recently. Leverkusen's attacking output remains decent, but defensive vulnerabilities are evident.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Recent encounters between these teams have been high-scoring, with their last meeting ending in a thrilling 3-3 draw. Over the last five head-to-heads, there have been over 2.5 goals in most matches, suggesting an open game, though Bremen have not lost in their last three against Leverkusen.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Leverkusen, sitting 6th with 29 points, have scored 34 goals (5th in the league) but underperformed their xG by -4.05, indicating finishing issues. Bremen, in 13th with 18 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 34 goals, and their attack has been inconsistent with just 21 goals scored.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Leverkusen, Patrik Schick (6 goals) and Alejandro Grimaldo (5 goals, 4 assists) are key threats despite recent team struggles. Bremen’s Romano Schmid, with 2 goals and 5 assists, has been influential in creating chances, ranking high in key passes league-wide.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Leverkusen dominate possession (59.5% avg.) and create big chances (62, 2nd in league), likely controlling the game at BayArena. Bremen’s late-game scoring (26% of goals after 76 mins) could exploit Leverkusen’s recent tendency to concede first and struggle defensively.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Leverkusen’s superior squad and home advantage, coupled with Bremen’s ongoing winless streak, the Sofascore prediction of a 60% home win probability seems reasonable. However, Bremen’s resilience in recent head-to-heads suggests they could nick a goal. Expect Leverkusen to edge it narrowly.
